description: Lock Amphora Shipping Standards to source-backed Roman/Mediterranean transport-amphora type standards, not generic storage jars or maritime law.

checks.reason: The corrected node is anchored to a source-backed Roman transport-amphora type range beginning in the middle of the first century BCE rather than the old Classical era-default placeholder.
Transport amphorae are ceramic vessels, so ceramic forming technology remains enabling infrastructure.
Amphora type standards scale through trade routes carrying commodities across the Mediterranean.
Maritime legal customs regulate shipping disputes but do not enable amphora shapes, capacities, or cargo-container standards.
The older generic storage-amphora node remains separate and should not be merged into this narrower classical/Roman standards node.
